description_long: This object is an abstract, stylized representation of a traffic controller, resembling a human figure in a dynamic pose. The form is highly angular and faceted, with elongated limbs and a torso that appear to be constructed from metallic, reflective surfaces in gold and bronze hues. The figure stands on one leg, with the other leg bent and one arm raised, possibly mimicking the gesture of directing traffic. There are no visible facial features, clothing details, or standard traffic signal lights, making the design more sculptural than functional. The surface is smooth and glossy, with sharp edges and a highly polished finish that reflects light. The overall impression is of a modern art installation or a conceptual urban sculpture rather than a conventional traffic signal.
description: A highly stylized, angular metallic sculpture of a traffic controller in a dynamic pose, featuring gold and bronze reflective surfaces and a smooth, glossy finish.
description_view_0: Close-up of the upper body and arm, highlighting the sharp, angular gold and bronze surfaces and the reflective, faceted design.
description_view_1: Side view showing the elongated limbs and the dynamic, one-legged stance, with the raised arm extending outward.
description_view_2: Distant, top-down view revealing the full figure, including the bent leg and the overall abstract, sculptural form.
description_view_3: Partial side/rear view focusing on the lower body and the base, with the curved leg and the reflective metallic surfaces visible.
attribute_light_configuration: No visible lights or standard traffic signal configuration present; purely sculptural with no signal indicators.
